New York Broadway Plaza, Proposed pedestrian transit mall, Times Square : environmental impact statement.

The Broadway Plaza project proposed transforming a section of Times Square in New York City into a pedestrian and transit-friendly area. The plan involved closing Broadway to vehicles between 45th and 48th Streets, creating three pedestrian plazas with amenities like new paving, trees, and information kiosks. The project aimed to improve pedestrian circulation, enhance the area's aesthetic appeal, and boost economic development by attracting tourists and businesses. However, it also raised concerns about increased traffic on surrounding streets and potential air quality issues.
Times Square, New York City, New York
Office of the Mayor, City of New York
New York City Department of Transportation
Office of Midtown Planning and Development

The Broadway Plaza project was proposed to enhance pedestrian access and improve the overall experience in the bustling Times Square area. While many supported the idea for its potential to beautify the space and promote tourism, there were concerns from local residents and businesses about the impact on traffic and air quality in surrounding areas. Ultimately, the project was implemented, leading to significant changes in the way people interact with this iconic location.
United States. Urban Mass Transportation Administration

"The absence of any clear focal point in Times Square is a loss to both the visitors to the space, as well as to the City for which, historically, it has been a major attraction." — Need for Action – Section I

"Broadway Plaza will provide a strong incentive to business investment in the area, which is seen as the means toward achieving the permanent rejuvenation of Times Square." — Socioeconomic – Section IV
